xanesnet.models.schnet package¶

Public API for the SchNet model.

class xanesnet.models.schnet.SchNet(model_type, hidden_channels, reduce_channels_1, reduce_channels_2, num_filters, num_interactions, num_gaussians, cutoff, mean_spectrum)[source]¶

Bases: Model

SchNet continuous-filter convolutional neural network for molecular property prediction.

Adapted for XANES spectral prediction. Architecture follows the original SchNet paper with interaction blocks built from continuous-filter convolutions (CFConv) and ShiftedSoftplus activations.

Note

Paper: “SchNet: A Continuous-filter Convolutional Neural Network for Modeling Quantum Interactions” (https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.08566). Implementation adapted from PyTorch Geometric’s SchNet (https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/2.5.3/_modules/ torch_geometric/nn/models/schnet.html).

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type identifier string.

  • hidden_channels (int) – Atom embedding dimension used throughout all interaction blocks.

  • reduce_channels_1 (int) – Output dimension of the first post-interaction linear layer.

  • reduce_channels_2 (int) – Output dimension of the second post-interaction linear layer (equals the spectral output size).

  • num_filters (int) – Number of filters in the CFConv layers and filter MLP.

  • num_interactions (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • num_gaussians (int) – Number of Gaussian basis functions for distance encoding.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance. Angstrom.

  • mean_spectrum (list[float] | None) – Optional reference mean spectrum for residual learning. If provided, it is added back on every forward pass after the output head. Its length must equal reduce_channels_2. (reduce_channels_2,)

__init__(model_type, hidden_channels, reduce_channels_1, reduce_channels_2, num_filters, num_interactions, num_gaussians, cutoff, mean_spectrum)[source]¶

Initialize SchNet.

forward(z, edge_index, edge_weight, batch=None)[source]¶

Run a forward pass through SchNet.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers. (num_atoms,)

  • edge_index (Tensor) – Edge indices (source, target). (2, num_edges)

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Interatomic distances. (num_edges,) Angstrom.

  • batch (Optional[Tensor]) – Batch assignment indices mapping each atom to a graph. (num_atoms,) Accepted for interface compatibility with batched PyG models, but ignored by this node-level SchNet variant because it returns per-atom outputs.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom output vectors. (num_atoms, reduce_channels_2)

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize all learnable layer weights and biases.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Weight initialization scheme name (looked up via WeightInitRegistry).

  • bias_init (str) – Bias initialization scheme name (looked up via BiasInitRegistry).

  • **kwargs – Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the weight initializer.

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return the model signature as a Config.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.schnet.schnet module¶

SchNet continuous-filter convolutional neural network for XANES prediction.

class xanesnet.models.schnet.schnet.SchNet(model_type, hidden_channels, reduce_channels_1, reduce_channels_2, num_filters, num_interactions, num_gaussians, cutoff, mean_spectrum)[source]¶

Bases: Model

SchNet continuous-filter convolutional neural network for molecular property prediction.

Adapted for XANES spectral prediction. Architecture follows the original SchNet paper with interaction blocks built from continuous-filter convolutions (CFConv) and ShiftedSoftplus activations.

Note

Paper: “SchNet: A Continuous-filter Convolutional Neural Network for Modeling Quantum Interactions” (https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.08566). Implementation adapted from PyTorch Geometric’s SchNet (https://pytorch-geometric.readthedocs.io/en/2.5.3/_modules/ torch_geometric/nn/models/schnet.html).

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type identifier string.

  • hidden_channels (int) – Atom embedding dimension used throughout all interaction blocks.

  • reduce_channels_1 (int) – Output dimension of the first post-interaction linear layer.

  • reduce_channels_2 (int) – Output dimension of the second post-interaction linear layer (equals the spectral output size).

  • num_filters (int) – Number of filters in the CFConv layers and filter MLP.

  • num_interactions (int) – Number of interaction blocks.

  • num_gaussians (int) – Number of Gaussian basis functions for distance encoding.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance. Angstrom.

  • mean_spectrum (list[float] | None) – Optional reference mean spectrum for residual learning. If provided, it is added back on every forward pass after the output head. Its length must equal reduce_channels_2. (reduce_channels_2,)

__init__(model_type, hidden_channels, reduce_channels_1, reduce_channels_2, num_filters, num_interactions, num_gaussians, cutoff, mean_spectrum)[source]¶

Initialize SchNet.

forward(z, edge_index, edge_weight, batch=None)[source]¶

Run a forward pass through SchNet.

Parameters:

  • z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers. (num_atoms,)

  • edge_index (Tensor) – Edge indices (source, target). (2, num_edges)

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Interatomic distances. (num_edges,) Angstrom.

  • batch (Optional[Tensor]) – Batch assignment indices mapping each atom to a graph. (num_atoms,) Accepted for interface compatibility with batched PyG models, but ignored by this node-level SchNet variant because it returns per-atom outputs.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom output vectors. (num_atoms, reduce_channels_2)

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize all learnable layer weights and biases.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Weight initialization scheme name (looked up via WeightInitRegistry).

  • bias_init (str) – Bias initialization scheme name (looked up via BiasInitRegistry).

  • **kwargs – Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the weight initializer.

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return the model signature as a Config.

class xanesnet.models.schnet.schnet.InteractionBlock(hidden_channels, num_gaussians, num_filters, cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Single SchNet interaction block.

Applies a continuous-filter convolution (CFConv) followed by a ShiftedSoftplus activation and a linear projection.

Parameters:

  • hidden_channels (int) – Atom embedding and hidden feature dimension.

  • num_gaussians (int) – Number of Gaussian basis functions (input width to the filter MLP).

  • num_filters (int) – Number of filters in the CFConv and filter MLP layers.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance. Angstrom.

__init__(hidden_channels, num_gaussians, num_filters, cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize InteractionBlock.

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize weights and biases for all sub-layers.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Weight initialization scheme name.

  • bias_init (str) – Bias initialization scheme name.

  • **kwargs – Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the weight initializer.

Return type:

None

forward(x, edge_index, edge_weight, edge_attr)[source]¶

Run the interaction block forward pass.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Atom feature matrix. (num_atoms, hidden_channels)

  • edge_index (Tensor) – Edge indices. (2, num_edges)

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Interatomic distances. (num_edges,) Angstrom.

  • edge_attr (Tensor) – Gaussian-smeared distances. (num_edges, num_gaussians)

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated atom features. (num_atoms, hidden_channels)

class xanesnet.models.schnet.schnet.CFConv(in_channels, out_channels, num_filters, net, cutoff)[source]¶

Bases: MessagePassing

Continuous-filter convolutional layer (message-passing variant).

Applies a filter network to edge attributes to produce per-edge weights, multiplied by a cosine envelope, and aggregates weighted neighbor features via sum pooling.

Parameters:

  • in_channels (int) – Input node feature dimension.

  • out_channels (int) – Output node feature dimension.

  • num_filters (int) – Intermediate filter dimension used by both linear projections.

  • net (Sequential) – Filter network mapping Gaussian-smeared distances to per-edge filter weights.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance for the cosine envelope. Angstrom.

__init__(in_channels, out_channels, num_filters, net, cutoff)[source]¶

Initialize CFConv.

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize linear layer weights and biases.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Weight initialization scheme name.

  • bias_init (str) – Bias initialization scheme name.

  • **kwargs – Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the weight initializer.

Return type:

None

forward(x, edge_index, edge_weight, edge_attr)[source]¶

Run the CFConv forward pass.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Node features. (num_nodes, in_channels)

  • edge_index (Tensor) – Edge indices. (2, num_edges)

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Interatomic distances. (num_edges,) Angstrom.

  • edge_attr (Tensor) – Gaussian-smeared distances. (num_edges, num_gaussians)

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated node features. (num_nodes, out_channels)

message(x_j, W)[source]¶

Compute messages as element-wise product of neighbor features and filter weights.

Parameters:

  • x_j (Tensor) – Source node features. (num_edges, num_filters)

  • W (Tensor) – Per-edge filter weights. (num_edges, num_filters)

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Messages. (num_edges, num_filters)

class xanesnet.models.schnet.schnet.GaussianSmearing(start=0.0, stop=5.0, num_gaussians=50)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Gaussian smearing layer for encoding scalar distances.

Expands scalar distance values into a vector of Gaussian basis function evaluations evenly spaced between start and stop.

Parameters:

  • start (float) – Center of the first Gaussian basis function. Angstrom.

  • stop (float) – Center of the last Gaussian basis function. Angstrom.

  • num_gaussians (int) – Number of evenly-spaced Gaussian basis functions. Must be at least 2.

__init__(start=0.0, stop=5.0, num_gaussians=50)[source]¶

Initialize GaussianSmearing.

forward(dist)[source]¶

Expand distances into Gaussian basis representation.

Parameters:

dist (Tensor) – Interatomic distances. (num_edges,) Angstrom.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Gaussian basis encodings. (num_edges, num_gaussians)

class xanesnet.models.schnet.schnet.ShiftedSoftplus[source]¶

Bases: Module

Shifted Softplus activation function.

Computes softplus(x) - log(2) so that the output is zero at x = 0, matching the shift used in the SchNet paper.

__init__()[source]¶

Initialize ShiftedSoftplus.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply the shifted softplus activation.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input tensor.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Activated tensor with the same shape as x.
